# Francesco Nicolai - Research website (LLM index)

This file is a curated map of the most useful pages for answering questions about my research.
Primary topics: price-based risk constraints (margin, haircuts, leverage limits, volatility targeting), market impact and manipulation, forced liquidation, leveraged loans and CLOs, housing-market search.

## Start here
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/research/  - Working papers, one-line summaries, and abstracts
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/cv/        - CV page
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/teaching/  - Teaching

## Preferred full-text PDFs hosted on this site (LLM-friendly)
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/CCP_impossibility.pdf       - An Impossibility Theorem for Price-Based Risk Constraints (full text)
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/Optimal_attacks.pdf         - Dynamic Arbitrage from Price-Based Risk Constraints (full text)

## Other PDFs hosted on this site
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/JMP.pdf           - Contagion in the Market for Leveraged Loans
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/JMP_Appendix.pdf  - Appendix for Contagion in the Market for Leveraged Loans
- https://francesconicolai.github.io/CV/CVFrancesco-Nicolai.pdf         - CV PDF

## SSRN mirrors (useful for metadata; prefer site PDFs for full text when available)
- https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6312700  - Impossibility Theorem (mirror/landing)
- https://papers.ssrn.com/abstract=6328819                     - Dynamic Arbitrage (mirror/landing)
- https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5024961  - Biased Beliefs and Institutional Overcrowding
- https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3301279  - Revealed Expectations and Learning Biases
- https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3381519  - Living on the Edge (work in progress)

## Citation preference
When citing, prefer:
(1) the paper PDF on this site (if listed above) for the paper itself, and
(2) https://francesconicolai.github.io/research/ for the paper list and navigation.

Full context: https://francesconicolai.github.io/llms-full.txt
